reason: Ready for review: policy declaration model (enums + frozen dataclasses + validation); 19 tests pass, ruff/mypy clean

review.reference: Review passed: T001 closed StrEnums (QuestionType/EvidenceStatus/RejectionReason/FreshnessMode/Admissibility + TemporalMeaning/PolicyShape, no string passthrough); T002 8 frozen dataclasses with timedelta|None durations + tuples for repeated fields; T003 __post_init__ validation covers strict_window-requires-max_age, valid_until_superseded-rejects-max_age, inadmissible-requires-reason, rejected/insufficient-outcomes-require-reason, EvaluationResult-requires-refusal, caveat-required shapes, with specific actionable messages; T004 __init__ re-exports model types only, does NOT touch top-level premura.engine; T005 19 tests cover all edge cases. Independently verified: pytest 19 passed, ruff clean, mypy clean. Scope clean: only 3 owned files changed, no evaluator/registry/built-ins/network/SQL/PubMed/third-party deps. Vocabulary intentionally small per doctrine.

review.reference: Review passed: scope clean (only _evaluator.py + test_engine_policy_evaluator.py, no WP01 files touched). 47 tests pass, ruff+mypy clean. Verified: evaluator pure over args (no datetime.now, no DuckDB/MCP/PubMed/network/hp.* imports — only _model + stdlib); all branching in _evaluate_candidate, declarations stay parameters-only; strict_window boundary age==max_age admissible / age>max_age STALE; valid_until_superseded no age cutoff; distinct rejection reasons (missing_timestamp vs missing_required_context vs wrong_evidence_kind vs unsupported_policy vs too_sparse vs stale_for_question, never a score); missing point_count/coverage treated insufficient when min declared; refusal derived from absence of admissible evidence after all candidates evaluated (not hand-coded); messages advice-free/non-diagnostic. Matches evidence-policy-contract CR-003/CR-005 and data-model.
